emails.subject: URGENT - Water leaking through ceiling in bedroom
emails.body:
Hi,

I woke up this morning and there is water actively dripping through the ceiling in my bedroom. It's coming from the light fitting which is really worrying me. I've put a bucket under it but the drip is getting worse. The carpet is already soaked through.

I'm in Apt 14B on the 3rd floor. I think it's coming from the apartment above me (15B). I knocked on their door but nobody answered.

Can someone come out ASAP? I'm worried about the electrics with water coming through the light.

Eoin Byrne
07700 100 200

emails.subject: Re: URGENT - Water leaking through ceiling in bedroom
emails.body:
Hi Dublin Maintenance team,

We have an active water leak at citynorth Quarter, Apt 14B (3rd floor). Water is coming through the ceiling light fitting from the unit above (15B). Tenant reports it's worsening.

This needs emergency attendance — potential electrical hazard. Can you confirm earliest availability this morning?

Property: citynorth Quarter
Access: Concierge on site, tenant is home
Tenant contact: Eoin Byrne, 07700 100 200

Thanks,
Tara

emails.subject: Re: URGENT - Water leaking through ceiling in bedroom
emails.body:
Tara,

We can have someone on-site by 10:30am. I'm sending Niall who knows the building well. He'll need access to both 14B and 15B — can you arrange keysafe access for 15B if the tenant isn't there?

Also just to flag, if this is a burst pipe in 15B we'll likely need to isolate the water supply to that unit. Might affect the units either side temporarily.

I'll send a quote once Niall has assessed but for reference our emergency callout is €180 + VAT before parts.

Barry

emails.subject: Q1 Occupancy & Arrears Report - citynorth Quarter
emails.body:
Hi Tara,

Hope you're well. We have our quarterly board meeting on March 18th and I'll need the following ahead of that:

1. Current occupancy rate and breakdown by unit type (studio, 1-bed, 2-bed)
2. Rent arrears summary — total outstanding, number of tenants in arrears, and aging breakdown (30/60/90 days)
3. Void turnaround times for Q1 — average days from move-out to new tenancy start

Could you have this over by March 12th? Happy to jump on a call if easier to walk through any of it.

Thanks,
Fiona

emails.subject: Heating not working - 3 days now
emails.body:
To whom it may concern,

I've been trying to get this sorted for three days now. My heating has completely stopped working since Monday. I reported it through the app on Monday evening and got an automated response saying someone would be in touch within 24 hours. Nobody has contacted me.

I called the office on Tuesday and was told the maintenance team would call me back. They didn't.

It's March and my apartment is freezing. I have a 6-month-old baby and the temperature in the flat is dropping to about 13 degrees at night. I've had to buy a portable heater which is costing me a fortune in electricity.

This is now a health and safety issue. I need this fixed TODAY or I will be contacting the RTB.

Emer Crowley
Apt 22A, reds Works

emails.subject: Viewing request - 2 bed apartment
emails.body:
Hi there,

I saw your listing on Daft for a 2-bed apartment at reds Works, €2,400/month. My partner and I are relocating from Cork for work (both starting new jobs in the IFSC in April).

We'd love to arrange a viewing. We're flexible on times but could do any evening this week or Saturday morning. We're pre-approved for references and can provide employer letters.

A few questions:
- Is parking included or is it extra?
- What's the lease term — 12 months minimum?
- Are pets allowed? We have a small dog (cockapoo, about 8kg)
- Is the BER rating available?

Look forward to hearing from you.

Lorcan & Emer

emails.subject: Noise complaint - ongoing party in apartment above
emails.body:
Hello,

It is 2am and the apartment above me (9C) has been having an extremely loud party since about 10pm. There is loud music, shouting, and what sounds like furniture being moved around or people jumping.

I have work at 6am and I've already knocked on their door twice. The first time they turned it down for about 20 minutes then it went back up louder. The second time nobody answered.

This is the third weekend in a row this has happened. I've been keeping a log:
- Feb 21: Party until 3:30am, music and shouting
- Feb 28: Loud music from 11pm-2am
- Tonight: Started at 10pm, still going

I need this addressed properly. A warning email isn't enough anymore. What are my options here?

Marek Kowalski
Apt 8C

emails.subject: Overnight incident report - March 5/6
emails.body:
Morning Tara,

Overnight report for your review:

1. FIRE ALARM ACTIVATION - 11:47pm
False alarm triggered on 4th floor. Fire panel showed detector in common hallway near Apt 16A. FRS attended, confirmed false alarm. Suspected cause: cooking smoke (tenant in 16A confirmed). Reset at 12:22am. Full building evacuation was not required but several residents complained about the alarm duration.

2. ANTISOCIAL BEHAVIOUR - 1:15am
Two males attempting to enter building via car park entrance. Not residents. Asked to leave, became verbally aggressive. Departed after I called Gardai. No physical confrontation. CCTV footage saved (camera 7 and 12).

3. MAINTENANCE - 3:30am
Tenant in Apt 2A (Mrs. Corcoran, elderly) called down reporting no hot water. I checked the boiler room — communal heating system showing an error code (E47). Wasn't able to reset. Logged for morning maintenance.

4. DELIVERY
Large furniture delivery arrived at 6:15am for Apt 19B. Driver insisted on using the main entrance. Directed to loading bay as per policy.

All incidents logged in the system. Let me know if you need anything else.

Donal

emails.subject: RTB Case Ref: DR-2026-0847 - Dispute Notification
emails.body:
Dear Mr. Forde,

This is to formally notify you that a dispute has been filed with the Residential Tenancies Board under reference DR-2026-0847.

Property: reds Works, Apt 31C
Tenant: Mr. Sean Morrissey
Landlord/Agent: ManageCo (on behalf of HMD Properties)

Nature of dispute: The tenant alleges that essential repairs (persistent damp and mould in the bedroom) have not been addressed despite repeated reports over a 4-month period, constituting a breach of the landlord's obligations under Section 12 of the Residential Tenancies Act 2004.

Yours sincerely,
Teresa Griffin

Residential Tenancies Board

emails.body:

Relocation Manager, TechCorp Ireland

emails.subject: Lease renewal terms - not happy
emails.body:
Declan,

I received the lease renewal letter yesterday and I'm honestly shocked. You're proposing a rent increase from €1,950 to €2,150 — that's over 10%.

As I understand it, rents in RPZs are capped at 2% per year. My current lease started in March 2025, so any increase should be capped at 2% which would be €1,989.

Can you explain how you've arrived at €2,150? I've checked the RTB rent pressure zone calculator and reds Works is definitely in a designated RPZ.

I want to renew — I like living here — but I won't be signing at that rate. Please come back to me with the correct figure.

Dara

emails.subject: Re: Lease renewal terms - not happy
emails.body:
Hi Dara,

Thanks for getting back to me on this. You raise a fair point and I want to make sure we get this right.

I'm going to review the calculation with our lettings team today. The increase was based on figures provided by the landlord, but you're correct that RPZ rules apply and we need to ensure full compliance.

I'll come back to you by end of day Friday with the confirmed renewal terms. In the meantime, your current lease terms remain in place — there's no pressure to sign anything immediately.

Apologies for any concern this has caused.

Best,
Declan

emails.subject: Graylings - Weekly clean schedule change request

Also wanted to flag that the bin store on the ground floor is getting out of hand again. Thanks,
Triona
GreenClean Services

emails.subject: New fire safety regulations - action required by April 1st
emails.body:
Hi all,

Just came out of the fire safety briefing with our compliance team. Key changes taking effect April 1st:

1. All BTR buildings over 6 storeys now require quarterly fire door inspections (was annual). We need to get inspection contracts in place ASAP.

2. Emergency lighting test frequency increases to monthly (from quarterly).

3. New requirement for fire safety information packs for all tenants — must be provided at move-in and annually thereafter. Template being prepared by legal.

4. Fire safety register must be digitised and accessible remotely by April 1st. Paper logs no longer sufficient.

Can each PM confirm by March 10th:
- Current fire door inspection status at your property
- Whether your emergency lighting testing is up to date
- Number of units requiring the new info pack

This is a compliance deadline — we can't miss it.

Shauna

emails.subject: Complaint about furniture delivery policy
emails.body:
Good morning,

I had a sofa delivered this morning and the concierge wouldn't let the delivery men use the main entrance. They had to carry a 3-seater sofa all the way around to the loading bay at the back of the building in the rain.

The delivery team almost dropped it and the sofa got wet. I understand there are policies but this is ridiculous. The main entrance has double doors — it would have taken 5 minutes.

Where is this delivery policy written? It wasn't in my tenancy agreement. I want to see the building rules document because I don't recall agreeing to this.

Also, the service lift was out of order (AGAIN) so they had to use the main lift, which meant holding it for 15 minutes while other residents waited. Not my fault the service lift isn't maintained.

Donnacha Hayes
Apt 19B

emails.subject: Planned water shutdown - March 12th, 9am-2pm

Dear Residents,

Please be advised that there will be a planned water shutdown at Ilah Residences on Wednesday, March 12th, from 9:00am to 2:00pm.

This is to carry out essential maintenance on the main water supply valve. During this time, there will be no cold or hot water supply to any apartment.

- Planning showers/baths before 9am

I've been wiping it down with mould spray weekly but it keeps coming back. My partner has developed a persistent cough and our GP said it could be related to mould exposure.

emails.subject: reds Works Lift 2 - Recurring fault analysis
emails.body:
Declan,

Following the third callout to Lift 2 in 30 days, I wanted to give you an update on what we're finding.

The OL-14 error code that keeps triggering relates to the overload sensor. Our engineer has identified that:

1. The load sensor calibration is drifting — it's triggering the overload protection at approximately 60% of actual capacity
2. The controller board is showing intermittent faults on the floor-selection circuit
3. Door operator motor is showing increased current draw, suggesting worn brushes

Our recommendation:
- Immediate: Recalibrate load sensor (included in service contract)
- Short-term: Replace controller board — €4,200 + labour
- Short-term: Replace door operator motor — €1,800 + labour
- Total estimate for parts and labour: €7,650 + VAT

Alternatively, given the lift is 14 years old, you may want to consider a full modernisation. We could provide a quote for that if interested.

The lift is safe to operate after today's recalibration, but the other faults will likely cause further outages without the parts replacement.

Let me know how you'd like to proceed.

Andrew Morton
Service Manager, Schindler Ireland

emails.subject: Re: Your query - Heating obligation
emails.body:
Dear Grainne,

Thank you for contacting Threshold. We've reviewed the details you've provided regarding the heating failure at your apartment.

Under Section 12(1)(b) of the Residential Tenancies Act 2004, your landlord is obligated to maintain the property in a proper state of repair. The Housing (Standards for Rented Houses) Regulations 2019 specifically require that heating facilities are maintained in good repair and working order.

A heating failure lasting 3 days, particularly with a young infant in the property, is a serious breach. We recommend:

1. Send a written notice to your landlord/agent by email (for a paper trail) detailing the issue, the dates reported, and the lack of response
2. Set a reasonable deadline (48 hours given the circumstances)
3. If not resolved, you can refer the matter to the RTB for dispute resolution
4. You may also contact your local authority's Environmental Health Officer, as this could constitute a housing standards breach

We're CC'ing your management company on this email for their attention.

Warm regards,
Threshold Housing Advice

Brendan
Apt 2C

emails.subject: Tenant event - April community BBQ
emails.body:

graystone wants to fund a community BBQ event for citynorth Quarter residents in April (weather permitting — maybe April 19th?).

We're thinking:

emails.subject: Re: citynorth - Communal boiler error E47 - urgent
emails.body:
Tara,

Niall's looked at both issues:

1. CEILING LEAK (Apt 14B): It's a burst flexi-hose under the kitchen sink in 15B. We've isolated and replaced it. Niall will run a dehumidifier in 14B to dry the ceiling. Might need a painter in a few days once it's dry. Total for the hose repair: €95 + callout.

2. COMMUNAL BOILER (E47 error): This is more involved. The primary heat exchanger is showing a blockage. We've managed a temporary reset but it will likely fault again within 24-48 hours. The heat exchanger needs a powerflush or possible replacement.

- Powerflush option: €650 + VAT, can do tomorrow morning
- Heat exchanger replacement: €2,800 + VAT, 3-day lead time on the part

My recommendation: Do the powerflush first. If that clears the blockage, you're good. If not, we'll need the replacement.

Hot water is restored for now but may drop out again.

Barry

emails.subject: Rent reduction request during lift outages
emails.body:
Declan,

I'm on the 9th floor and Lift 2 has been out of service three times this month alone. Combined with the times Lift 1 has been crowded because it's the only one working, I've been late to work twice because I couldn't get downstairs in time.

For a building charging premium rents, having a single working lift for 300+ residents is not acceptable. I didn't sign up for climbing 9 flights of stairs.

I'd like to formally request a rent reduction for any month where lift service is materially disrupted. I'm not talking about occasional maintenance — I mean the repeated breakdowns we've been experiencing.

I've spoken to several neighbours and they feel the same way. If this isn't addressed, we'll be raising it collectively.

Gavin
Apt 25A

emails.body:

Following up on our conversation at PropTech Connect, please find our proposal for EV charging infrastructure at citynorth Quarter.

emails.subject: Smells from neighbouring apartment
emails.body:
Hi Maeve,

This is a sensitive one and I'm not sure how to handle it.

There's a strong, unpleasant smell coming from the apartment next door (13A). It's been getting worse over the past week. It seems to come through the shared wall, particularly in the hallway.

I don't want to cause trouble for my neighbour, but the smell is affecting my quality of life. I've tried air fresheners and opening windows but it just comes back.

I also haven't seen the tenant from 13A in over a week, which is unusual — she's normally in and out. Her post is piling up outside the door.

I'm probably overthinking this but given the smell and the fact I haven't seen her... should someone check on her?

Sienna
Apt 13B

emails.body:
Ronan,

The tenant in Apt 4C is smoking inside their apartment and the smell is coming into mine through the vents and what I think is a gap around the pipes under the kitchen sink.

I chose a non-smoking building specifically because I have asthma. The smell is worst in the evenings and at weekends. It's making my eyes water and I've had to use my inhaler more frequently.

I've checked my lease and it says smoking is prohibited inside all apartments. Can you enforce this? I don't want to confront my neighbour directly.

Eamon
Apt 4D

emails.subject: Following up on December water damage claim
emails.body:
Tara,

I'm following up on the water damage to my apartment from the burst pipe in December. It's been nearly 3 months and I still haven't been able to use my spare bedroom.

The ceiling was repaired in January but the carpet is still ruined and stinks of damp. I was told the insurance assessor would be in touch about replacing it. I've heard nothing.

My personal belongings that were damaged (laptop, books, photo albums) — the assessor told me to claim on my own contents insurance. My insurer is now asking for a letter from management confirming the cause of damage wasn't my fault.

Can you please:
1. Give me an update on the carpet replacement timeline
2. Provide the letter my insurer needs
3. Confirm when the skirting boards will be replaced (they're warped from the water)

I've been very patient but 3 months without a usable bedroom is not on.

Colm
Apt 15A

emails.body:

I act for Mr. Wayne Phelan, tenant at Apt 4B, Thornbury Village.

My client has instructed me to respond to the warning notice dated March 7th.

My client's position:
1. The alleged 'unauthorised occupants' are his brother and friend who stay occasionally but do not reside at the property permanently
2. The noise complaints are exaggerated and my client has never been spoken to about noise before receiving the formal warning

Key findings:

COMPLIANT:
- Fire detection and alarm system
- Emergency lighting (common areas)
- Fire extinguisher locations and servicing

NON-COMPLIANT (must be addressed within 28 days):

Dublin Fire Brigade, Fire Prevention Section

I've drafted a letter that 23 residents have already signed asking management to:
1. Make a formal submission to the planning process
2. Organise a residents' meeting about the development
3. Engage a planning consultant on our behalf

Also — on a practical note — I noticed the developer has started doing site surveys already. There were people with measuring equipment on the boundary yesterday. Are they allowed on our side of the boundary? I didn't think so.

Nuala
